spec_match.rationale: The demo exercises `text-overflow: ellipsis` on editable tag inputs and non-form focus targets, matching the CSS Overflow interaction hook and Chrome 148 release note.

interactive.rationale: Real controls add, edit, remove, reset, and probe tags while live status, overflow metrics, and JSON output update from user actions.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature-index blurb promises clipped editable tag chips, long-name additions, and tab/focus behavior; the page delivers those flows and adds related non-form coverage.

edge_cases.rationale: The page now surfaces empty-tag removal, duplicate rejection, long unbroken overflow, CSS support fallback messaging, and non-form focus targets.

references.rationale: A labelled references section links directly to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5146265241387008 plus the relevant CSS Overflow and Chrome 148 sources.

design_system.rationale: All added colours use existing CSS variables, button background overrides keep explicit text colours, and the page remains an interactive demo rather than a static card.

summary: The inline tag editor now closes the prior major self-heal gap with a ChromeStatus reference and adds explicit edge-case/support states around the original live tag editing workflow.
